qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Malformed WebP input crashes bundled WebP decoder
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a malformed WebP parser crash in `processWEBP`/`WebPDecoder`, not the advisory's node-only local file read/path traversal in `loadFile`/`fileloading.js`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause. The GHSA is about unsanitized path strings reaching Node file reads through `loadFile` and related methods, while the session finding is a malformed WebP content crash in the decoder.
evidence: The advisory details and fix commit describe node-only local file inclusion/path traversal caused by user-controlled paths passed into `loadFile`, with the patch adding file-system read restrictions in `src/modules/fileloading.js`.
The only qualifying finding reports an empty-frame dereference in the bundled WebP decoder after malformed RIFF/WEBP input reaches `processWEBP`/`WebPDecoder`.
Although both involve jsPDF image-related APIs such as `addImage`, the vulnerable code and root cause differ: file path authorization versus malformed image parsing.
